promotion_tool: governed dev→release promotion (copy allow-listed changed files + ZIP)
store_upload: NONE — no Chrome Web Store upload performed by this promotion
github_release: NONE — no GitHub Release created by this promotion
wildcard_host_permissions: NONE — AG-363 removed https://*/* from manifest.chrome.json and manifest.firefox.json (unchanged by this promotion)
note: Governed dev→release promotion of AG-PROMPT-381 (HR/Employee false-positive fix on engineering QA report text) on top of the AG-380 baseline, which already promoted AG-378 (PDF Author metadata precedence) and AG-379 (field-blind metadata confidential fix) into ZIP SHA 3c3926f3...; those two are NOT re-described here as pending — they are already shipped in the prior release state and remain unchanged and confirmed present in this promotion. Exactly one allow-listed file changed since the AG-380 promotion lineage (source commit 332f4c1): src/detection/packs/english.ts (confirmed via git diff against 332f4c1, restricted to the full AG-269 allow-list, and independently via a full recursive src/ tree diff between dev and release before this promotion — no other allow-listed file touched; public/manifest.chrome.json and public/manifest.firefox.json confirmed byte-identical between dev and release, no permission drift). AG-381 root cause (EMPLOYEE_ROSTER_KEYWORDS_TOO_WEAK): english-hr-compensation and english-hr-performance (english.ts) fired a High-severity 'HR/Employee data' warning on a single match of a common, polysemous English word (e.g. 'bonus' used generically, as in 'a bonus fix was also made', or 'termination' in a non-HR technical sense), with no minimum-match-count requirement — the identical gap class AG-PROMPT-350 already fixed for the sibling signal registry-hr-employee, not yet applied to these two. An operator engineering/CSS-QA report (local reproduction material, never committed) triggered this via a single incidental 'bonus' mention. Fixed by applying the same AG-350 precedent (minCount: 2) to both patterns, plus a necessary companion fix: both patterns were missing the regex 'g' flag, without which minCount could never be enforced via String.match() (confirmed against the already-working registry-hr-employee precedent, which already had 'g'). This is a detection-pattern precision fix only — no detection threshold structure, RiskSignal structure, deduplication, chunking logic, or severity architecture changed; no signal IDs added or removed; no CRITICAL severity or hard floor added; real HR/compensation/performance/roster/manager-reporting/employee-ID/staff-list detection remains fully covered by positive-control tests. AG-369/370/371/374/376/378/379 (previously promoted) remain included unchanged: handleDrop's event.stopImmediatePropagation() Copilot drag/drop Cancel fix, the Firefox Xray-wrapper realm-copy helper for DOCX/XLSX/PPTX extraction parity, the XLSX/PPTX extraction success-semantics fix, the nordic-financial-terms bare-'balance' false-positive fix, the global-dob spelled-out-month-date fix plus meta.author abbreviation/tool-name guard, and the field-aware metadata-confidential fix — all confirmed present via a byte-identical comparison of this promotion's release-built content.js/background.js against a same-session dev-built Chrome bundle (strongest available fix-presence proof; confirms the entire src/ tree, not just the one changed file, matches dev exactly), plus literal-string greps for 'bonus' (AG-381), 'jan(?:uary)?' (AG-378), and 'stopImmediatePropagation' (AG-369). No manifest, permission, or host-scope change (permissions: ['storage'] only; host_permissions limited to the 6 listed patterns; no activeTab; no wildcard/https://*/*; no <all_urls>; connect-src 'none'). No telemetry, network diagnostics, or centralized logging added; no raw document content, file names, or personal data logged or persisted (production bundle strips all console.log/console.debug via terser pure_funcs, verified; zero new diagnostic calls in the changed file). The local, real-world reproduction files used to diagnose AG-376 and AG-381 (docs/test-files/1.txt through 4.txt) were never committed, never quoted verbatim in any report, and are not part of this or any prior promotion; AG-381 used only synthetic fixtures in its committed tests. Dev-side verification (this promotion): tsc 0 errors; targeted keyword-precision suite 30/30; AG-378 PDF Author metadata suite 15/15; AG-379 metadata confidential field-awareness suite 19/19; CORE 55/55; corpus gold harness 30/0/1-blocked (pre-existing, unrelated); clipboard gold harness 125/125 (incl. re-verified english-hr-compensation-paste positive control); signal-manifest-integrity 24/24 (no registration drift, 93 pack pattern IDs / 101 manifest constants unchanged); verify-manifest 28/28; Chrome build 34/34 + 20/20 consumer-copy; Firefox build 25/25. Release-side verification: release-verify.ts 10/10 gates (type-check, build:chrome + artifact/console-strip checks, package:chrome + no-maps/no-TS guards, license UX guardrail, no-activity-record, message-handler safety, storage manager, storage hardening, signal-manifest integrity, manifest verification); ZIP contents confirmed as exactly the 14 expected production files (no .map/.ts/reports/dev URLs/prompt IDs/docs-test-files). No store upload; no GitHub Release; no website repo modified.
